texto: The poets are supposed to liberate the words – not chain them in phrases. Who told the poets they were supposed to think? Poets are meant to sing and to make words sing. Writers don't own their words. Since when do words belong to anybody? 'Your very own words,' indeed! And who are you?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Brion Gysin que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “The poets are supposed to liberate the words – not chain them in phrases. Who told the poets they…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, art, brion-gysin, com foco em brion-gysin e cut-ups-self-explained.

frase_original: The poets are supposed to liberate the words – not chain them in phrases. Who told the poets they were supposed to think? Poets are meant to sing and to make words sing. Writers don't own their words. Since when do words belong to anybody? 'Your very own words,' indeed! And who are you?

texto: The resulting texts always took a narrative term, enigmatic at first but ultimately explicit and often premonitory. The semantic distribution of these basic elements diverted them from their original meaning, thus revealing their real significance. Henceforth, every form of writing will consist of an operation of decoding, of contamination, and of sense perversion. All this because all language is essentially mystification, and everything is fiction.
explicacao: Citação de Brion Gysin sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “The resulting texts always took a narrative term, enigmatic at first but ultimately explicit and …” articula reflexao, code, contamination em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como contamination e cut-ups.
